empirical.interpretation: M0 locks the OFFICIAL BabyLM-2026 scorer (summed pseudo-log-likelihood, no length normalization) as the v0.2 inner-loop metric and reproduces v0.1 official scores exactly from disk via scripts/run_official_eval.py (Strict BLiMP 67.56, Strict-Small 59.46 — abs delta 0.0 vs EVAL_OUTCOMES_REPORT.md, well within the 0.5 tolerance), validating the wrapper + parser. The official/internal gap (vs paper 73.06/64.09) is the no-length-normalization scoring, documented in docs/memory/official_blimp_scoring.md. v0.1 is below the GPT-2 baseline officially, so the v0.2 climb is real. Layer-1 is green (667 pass / 3 skip, ruff+mypy clean, coverage 86.66%). Seed-stability levers (dropout 0.1, grad-clip 1.0, post-build reseed) are already in the recipe; CV(BLiMP) is measured per-backbone in M1's multi-seed bake-off (the one missing lever, Muon LR warmup, is added there if the CV gate is missed) — folded into M1 rather than a redundant standalone M0 GPU sweep.

integrity.tarka_memo: Strongest objection: 'the 67.56 reproduction is parsed from cached v0.1 result files, not a fresh eval run, so it proves the parser, not that the harness still loads the model the same way.' Resolution: the parser matches the independently-recorded EVAL_OUTCOMES_REPORT.md numbers exactly; the harness load itself was validated in the v0.1 session (smoke 68.08 approx full 67.56, self-consistent), and a fresh smoke run is available via `--stage smoke`. Second objection: 'folding seed-stability into M1 hides a known 100M instability.' Resolution: M1 measures CV on the chosen backbone at 10M (official scorer) and M4 confirms seed-robustness at 100M with best-of-N fallback before any submission; the levers are already present, so this is measurement-ordering, not omission.

_note: M0 is a FOUNDATION milestone. technical+empirical+integrity+memory are satisfied. artifacts.paper_section_updated and demos_run_clean are intentionally false: M0 produces no paper finding or demo (the finding is recorded in research/memory/findings.md F4 + ADR-0041 + docs/memory/official_blimp_scoring.md); the paper/demo bump and human sign-off are milestone-level events that land at the empirical milestones (M1+ and M4). `psalm contract check` will therefore list artifacts as outstanding for M0 by design.
